description: Sample couple and parent-child conversation transcripts for MindShift tone analysis testing

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: You said you'd take care of the dishes three hours ago. They're still sitting there.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I was going to get to them. You don't have to remind me every five minutes.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Every five minutes? I asked you once. Three hours ago. That's the problem — you don't even hear me.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Here we go again. Everything I do is wrong.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I'm not saying everything is wrong. I just need you to follow through when you say you'll do something.

conversations.scenario: Couple reconnecting after a disagreement about finances

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Hey, I've been thinking about what you said last night about the budget. You had some good points.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Really? Because it felt like you just shut down when I brought it up.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I know. I did shut down. I get overwhelmed when money comes up because it makes me feel like I'm not providing enough.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I didn't realize that's what was going on. I'm not saying you're not providing — I just want us to plan together.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: That makes sense. Can we sit down this weekend and look at everything together?

conversations.scenario: Parent setting boundaries with teenager about screen time

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Riley, it's 11 PM on a school night. You need to put the phone away.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: All my friends are still online. You're the only parent who does this.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I get that it feels unfair. But sleep matters, and tomorrow you have that math test.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Fine. But can I at least say goodnight to Avery?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Two minutes, then phone on the charger in the kitchen. Deal?

conversations.scenario: One partner stonewalling during discussion about visiting in-laws

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: My mom asked if we're coming for Easter. We need to give her an answer.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Mm-hmm.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Drew. Can you actually talk to me about this? I feel like I'm talking to a wall.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: What do you want me to say? You already know I don't want to go. But you're going to make us go anyway.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I don't want to make you do anything. But I need you to tell me what you're actually feeling instead of shutting down.

conversations.scenario: Parent comforting child after a bad day at school

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Nobody picked me for their team at recess today. Again.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Oh buddy, that sounds really painful. Come sit with me.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I hate recess. I just want to stay inside and read.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: It makes sense you'd want to avoid something that hurts. Being left out is one of the worst feelings.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Do you think there's something wrong with me?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: There is nothing wrong with you. Some kids are slower to find their people, and that's okay. Want to brainstorm some ideas together?

conversations.scenario: Partner stonewalls when asked about a job offer requiring relocation

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I got the offer from the Denver office today. We need to talk about this.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Cool.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Liam, this is a big deal. I need you to actually engage with me here.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: You've already decided. I can tell by the way you brought it up.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I haven't decided anything. I'm bringing it to you because I want us to decide together. But I can't do that if you won't talk.

conversations.scenario: Partner becomes emotionally flooded during conversation about trust after discovering a hidden credit card

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I found the credit card statement, Ethan. Six thousand dollars you never told me about.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I — I was going to tell you. I just — there's so much happening and I didn't know how to — I can't even think right now.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: You can't think? I'm the one who just found out my husband has been hiding debt from me!

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: I know, I know, I'm sorry, I'm so sorry. My heart is pounding. I feel like the room is spinning. Can we — can I just have five minutes?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Fine. Five minutes. But we are talking about this tonight. I'm not letting this get swept under the rug.

conversations.scenario: One partner uses contempt and criticism about the other's parenting choices

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: You let them have ice cream for dinner again? Seriously, it's like I'm raising three children instead of two.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: It was one night. They'd already eaten most of their food.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: One night? You do this every time I'm not here. You have zero discipline. The kids walk all over you.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: That's not fair, Nora. I'm a good dad. You don't get to talk to me like that.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: If you were consistent with them, I wouldn't have to.

conversations.scenario: Partner initiates repair the morning after an argument about social media boundaries

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: Hey. I made you coffee. Can we talk about last night?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I don't know if I'm ready. I'm still pretty upset.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: That's okay. I just want you to know I heard you. I was wrong to get defensive about the DMs. You had every right to be uncomfortable.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: It's not about the messages themselves. It's that you hid them. That's what hurt.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: You're right. I should have been upfront. I'll do better. Can we figure out together what feels okay for both of us?

conversations.scenario: Couple starts heated about division of childcare but successfully de-escalates

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: I'm exhausted, Chris. I've done every single bedtime this week. You just sit on the couch.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: That's not true — I did Tuesday. And I've been working late every night this week.

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: Okay, wait. I don't want this to turn into a scorekeeping fight. I'm just tired and I need more help.

turns.speaker: speaker_b
turns.text: You're right, I'm sorry. I know you're carrying a lot. What if I take bedtime Thursday through Sunday?

turns.speaker: speaker_a
turns.text: That would help a lot. Thank you for hearing me. I know you're tired too.
